subject: fix(boot): add 404.html fallback and inline loading indicator
body:
GitHub Pages returns its default 404 for any path that doesn't map to a
real file. After React Router navigates to /login or /projects (client-
side), a hard refresh (pulldown on mobile) asks GitHub Pages for that
path — which doesn't exist. Without a 404.html the response is empty,
producing the blank screen the user sees (no HTML, no JS, no DebugPill).

Changes:
- deploy.yml: copy index.html → 404.html after build so GitHub Pages
  serves the SPA shell for every route
- index.html: add an inline loading spinner inside #root that is visible
  immediately while JS downloads; React's createRoot().render() replaces
  #root's children, removing it automatically. Also add <noscript>
  fallback for disabled-JS browsers.
- usePWAUpdate.ts: prefix unused swUrl param to fix TS6133

Tags: deploy, spa-routing, pwa, ux
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: blank-screen-fix
Semver: patch

title: add 404.html fallback and inline loading indicator
